page_content:
CMSC466 Introduction to Numerical Analysis I (3 Credits) Floating point computations, direct methods for linear systems, interpolation, solution of nonlinear equations. Prerequisite: 1 course with a minimum grade of C- from (CMSC106, CMSC131); and minimum grade of C- in MATH410. Cross-listed with: AMSC466. Credit Only Granted for: AMSC460, CMSC460, AMSC466, or CMSC466.

CMSC452 Elementary Theory of Computation (3 Credits) Techniques are developed to determine the difﬁculty of a problem relative to a model of computation. Topics include Finite Automata, P, NP, decidability, undecidability, and communication complexity. Prerequisite: Minimum grade of C- in CMSC351; and permission of CMNS-Computer Science department. Or must be in the (Computer Science (Doctoral), Computer Science (Master's)) program.
